jokes.text: Oh, yeah! I've got a big box, yes I do! I've got a big box, how about you?
jokes.setup: Michael singing an improvised childish song about a delivery box
jokes.punchline: The unintended sexual innuendo of 'big box' delivered with complete innocence
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - completely oblivious to innuendo while being childishly excited. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: It is 'from Sah-bray,' our new owner, and it is 'to Sah-bray.' Us!
jokes.setup: Michael mispronouncing 'Sabre' as 'Sah-bray'
jokes.punchline: His confident delivery of the wrong pronunciation while trying to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Michael's pretentious mispronunciation perfectly captures his character - trying to be impressive while being wrong.

jokes.text: I wonder what's inside. Scissor me.
jokes.setup: Michael requesting scissors to open the box
jokes.punchline: The unintentionally sexual phrasing of 'scissor me'
jokes.explanation: Economical and perfectly in character - Michael's complete obliviousness to sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: We didn't need them touching our asses.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how Sabre saved them
jokes.punchline: Escalating the 'ass-saving' metaphor to an uncomfortable sexual image
jokes.explanation: Michael taking a common expression too literally, creating awkward imagery in his typical style.

jokes.text: This cord has Creed written all over it.
jokes.setup: Looking at office equipment being distributed
jokes.punchline: Creed's inexplicable claim of ownership over a random cord
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed - completely random, confident, and unexplained. High character integration with economy of delivery.

jokes.text: Due to circumstances beyond my control... Impulsivity and inattention to detail... Hey, hey.
jokes.setup: Michael blaming circumstances beyond his control
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately listing Michael's actual flaws that are totally within his control
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff structure with Jim calling out Michael's self-delusion in real time.

jokes.text: Impossible. He opened it like an ape.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether they can repack the box
jokes.punchline: Dwight's blunt, animalistic comparison of Michael's box-opening technique
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight - matter-of-fact insult delivered with complete seriousness.

jokes.text: Have you tried making everything smaller?
jokes.setup: Group trying to figure out how to repack the box
jokes.punchline: Kevin's childishly illogical solution to a physical problem
jokes.explanation: Peak Kevin logic - approaching a physical problem with impossible magical thinking, delivered earnestly.

jokes.text: No, don't! Thank you. Oh! Got it. Okay.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Erin to 'scissor me' again after they just repacked everything
jokes.punchline: Someone (likely Jim or Dwight) stopping him, followed by Michael's immediate reopening of the box
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier innuendo plus Michael's impulsive destruction of their hard work.

jokes.text: Why do I have to explain everything? Because we're usually not on the same page.
jokes.setup: Michael asking why he has to explain everything
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact explanation that they're never on the same page
jokes.explanation: Jim's deadpan honesty about their communication problems, delivered as helpful clarification.

jokes.text: Gabe seems tall. Hope we get along.
jokes.setup: Kevin assessing the new Sabre representative
jokes.punchline: Kevin linking height to compatibility in his unique logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin logic - connecting completely unrelated traits with earnest hope. Perfectly economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Welcome. Scranton hotdog from Scranton?
jokes.setup: Greeting the new corporate representative
jokes.punchline: Dwight's aggressive hospitality offering a geographically redundant local food
jokes.explanation: Dwight's overeager hospitality with an absurdly specific local offering.

jokes.text: Co-manager, actually. In training. Trained. Loving it. Good at it, so...
jokes.setup: Michael introducing Jim as his protégé
jokes.punchline: The rapid-fire corrections showing Michael's need to maintain authority while Jim asserts equality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael-Jim dynamic - Michael's protective authority vs Jim's quiet assertion of equality.

jokes.text: Sabre. Sabre. It's Sabre. It's Sabre. Oh. Of course. Sabre.
jokes.setup: Everyone mispronouncing the company name as 'Sah-bray'
jokes.punchline: The repeated corrections and Michael's fake understanding
jokes.explanation: Running gag about pronunciation, functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Dunder Mifflin and Sah-bray... Sabre. It's Sabre. Dunder Mifflin and Sabre
jokes.setup: Andy singing the welcome song
jokes.punchline: Continuing to mispronounce even while being corrected mid-song
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the pronunciation gag, gets repetitive but shows commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: Oh, you MapQuested it. It's four blocks away. Well, now we won't get lost. Or we could drive, and that takes one minute.
jokes.setup: Jim being overly prepared for a short trip
jokes.punchline: The absurd over-preparation for an extremely short distance
jokes.explanation: Gentle mockery of over-preparation, good relationship dynamic showing Jim's quirks.

jokes.text: But if not, there's always the Army. The infantry. Okay. Okay. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Discussing daycare options for their baby
jokes.punchline: Escalating from prestigious daycare to military service as backup childcare
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from daycare anxiety to military service, with Jim's increasing concern.

jokes.text: Sabre is respecting the past, but opening the window to the future.
jokes.setup: Corporate training video with celebrity spokesman
jokes.punchline: Meaningless corporate speak delivered with earnest celebrity endorsement
jokes.explanation: Parody of corporate nonsense-speak, elevated by the absurdity of celebrity endorsement.

jokes.text: Have you ever tasted a rainbow? At Sabre, you will.
jokes.setup: Corporate video making grand promises
jokes.punchline: Absurd sensory claim that makes no literal sense
jokes.explanation: Memorably absurd corporate promise - 'tasting a rainbow' is beautifully nonsensical.

jokes.text: Perfect. No. Don't like that.
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining the new business model
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate, blunt rejection after agreeing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - fake agreement followed by immediate honesty. Economy and timing are excellent.

jokes.text: Talk about vacation days!
jokes.setup: Gabe encouraging honest communication
jokes.punchline: Stanley immediately hijacking for his personal agenda
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley - completely ignoring company priorities for personal vacation concerns.

jokes.text: No. Don't like that.
jokes.setup: Learning about website blocking
jokes.punchline: Michael's continued blunt rejections of changes
jokes.explanation: Callback to his pattern of blunt rejection, economical but getting repetitive.

jokes.text: They're two-ounce paper cups dipped in plastic. He goes through 20 a day.
jokes.setup: Discussion about replacing water cups
jokes.punchline: Oscar's precise technical description of Michael's cup obsession
jokes.explanation: Oscar's accountant precision applied to Michael's silly habits - good character work.

jokes.text: You know what can't fit in a bottle are the 20 little trips I take to the cooler, and the 20 little scans I do of everybody to make sure everything's running smoothly, and the 20 little conversations that I have with Stanley.
jokes.setup: Defending his water cup usage
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate justification linking water cups to his management philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - connecting cup usage to his entire management style with escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: But 70% of me is water, and the other part, the real part, the part that has feelings and emotions and thoughts and makes decisions, and, if I can be crass, makes babies, that part thinks that all of these changes suck...
jokes.setup: Michael philosophizing about the water bottle change
jokes.punchline: Escalating from basic biology to reproduction while discussing office supplies
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael - pseudo-philosophical rambling escalating to inappropriate oversharing about reproduction.

jokes.text: You don't have enough water in there? Your stomach?
jokes.setup: Michael's elaborate water philosophy speech
jokes.punchline: Gabe's confused, literal response missing the entire philosophical point
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man work from Gabe, showing the disconnect between Michael's philosophizing and reality.

jokes.text: Too much change is not a good thing. Ask the climate.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about changes
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly relevant environmental reference
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly clever Michael line - using climate change as an analogy is unexpectedly apt and quotable.

jokes.text: Am I too old to go here?
jokes.setup: Jim looking at the daycare's kid-friendly features
jokes.punchline: Adult man genuinely considering if he's too old for daycare
jokes.explanation: Jim's childlike wonder at daycare amenities, sweet character moment.

jokes.text: Well, maybe they ran away 'cause the pizza was, like, 'Hey, get out of here, you stupid strawberries.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about missing strawberries on pizza
jokes.punchline: Kevin imagining pizza ingredients having a confrontational conversation
jokes.explanation: Peak Kevin - anthropomorphizing food ingredients with childish dialogue. Brilliantly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Thank you. I've been working on it since I was a little girl.
jokes.setup: Michael complimenting her accent
jokes.punchline: Unexpected revelation about working on accent since childhood, delivered by older Southern woman
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction - suggests both vanity and long-term commitment to image.

jokes.text: the last time I saw a company as mismanaged as Dunder Mifflin was my grandson's snowball company
jokes.setup: Jo criticizing Dunder Mifflin's management
jokes.punchline: Comparing professional company to child's play business
jokes.explanation: Devastating insult that's both folksy and brutal - perfect character establishment for Jo.

jokes.text: Is Christian Slater back there? Because he knows... He'd know what to do.
jokes.setup: Michael struggling with corporate authority
jokes.punchline: Appealing to celebrity from training video as business expert
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - trusting celebrity spokesman over actual executives. Perfect callback and character work.

jokes.text: No. Yes, we saw this, yes. No, it wasn't like a look around, we really just had a peek. Quick peek. Didn't focus on anything in particular. It's lovely.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if they saw the daycare already
jokes.punchline: Jim's increasingly awkward over-explanation making it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - the attempt to fix an awkward situation making it more awkward.

jokes.text: That's the weirdest possible solution to the problem.
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting they bring up the bathroom incident
jokes.punchline: Pam's direct assessment that addressing awkwardness directly is the worst option
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on awkward situation management, showing Pam's social awareness.

jokes.text: Hey! Wow. That's very loud. Keep it up, son!
jokes.setup: Meeting David Wallace's young son making noise
jokes.punchline: Michael's enthusiastic encouragement of disruptive behavior
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate encouragement of child's disruptive behavior, shows his boundary issues.

jokes.text: 'Cause I saw you had shoes on, so I didn't know if you did anything.
jokes.setup: Asking her husband about his day
jokes.punchline: Using shoes as indicator of leaving the house/depression level
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny insight into David's unemployed depression - shoes as sign of basic functioning.

jokes.text: Is this because Jim walked in on you going to the bathroom?
jokes.setup: Daycare interview going poorly
jokes.punchline: Pam directly addressing the elephant in the room
jokes.explanation: Pam doing exactly what she said was the weirdest solution - addressing the awkwardness directly.

jokes.text: This coming from the guy who still uses a children's toilet.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning their charm
jokes.punchline: Pam's brutal comeback about his bathroom setup
jokes.explanation: Devastating Pam comeback - unexpected aggression with perfect ammunition. Great escalation of the conflict.

jokes.text: Why didn't you just lock the door, man? It doesn't lock for the children's safety. Anybody could have walked in. It was story time.
jokes.setup: Discussing the bathroom incident
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity of the bathroom situation during story time
jokes.explanation: Escalating the awkward bathroom situation with more uncomfortable details.

jokes.text: So the kid's making the noise to pick it up? Yeah. No. No, no, that's the vacuum noise.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his toy vacuum invention
jokes.punchline: Confusion about who/what makes the vacuum sound
jokes.explanation: Confusion about a poorly thought-out invention, showing both characters' mental states.

jokes.text: That's as hard as I can hint.
jokes.setup: Erin trying to get Andy to ask her out
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall of her own hinting strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin line - meta-awareness of her own failed subtlety. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: 'Suck lt.' That's what it's called. Oh! Okay. Huh? Catchy. Kid's will love it.
jokes.setup: David naming his toy vacuum invention
jokes.punchline: The unintentionally sexual name delivered with complete innocence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate product name delivered with complete sincerity. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: There are very few things that would make me not want to team up with David Wallace, and Suck It is one of them.
jokes.setup: David offering Michael partnership in his invention
jokes.punchline: Michael's rare moment of good judgment about the inappropriate name
jokes.explanation: Rare moment where Michael shows better judgment than someone else, with perfect delivery.

jokes.text: That was some sort of weird creature that lives in David Wallace's house.
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on David's changed behavior
jokes.punchline: Describing unemployed David as literally a different species
jokes.explanation: Michael's characteristically dramatic but actually insightful observation about David's transformation.

jokes.text: You know when people say getting fired was the best thing that ever happened to them? I feel sorry for those people. That? The best thing? Really?
jokes.setup: David reflecting on unemployment
jokes.punchline: Questioning the cliché about job loss being positive
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of the 'getting fired was great' trope, showing David's genuine struggle.

jokes.text: Orange juice is in here, and like Sabre, it is from Florida, and it is good.
jokes.setup: Michael attempting a toast
jokes.punchline: Connecting orange juice to corporate metaphor in the most literal way possible
jokes.explanation: Michael's hilariously literal corporate metaphor-making, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Just 'cause you have liquid, that doesn't make it a toast.
jokes.setup: Michael's non-toast with orange juice
jokes.punchline: Jim's technical critique of toast requirements
jokes.explanation: Jim's practical deconstruction of Michael's toast attempt, good straight-man work.

jokes.text: Wow, that is metallicy. That's like drinking a battery. Really gets you in the fillings, doesn't it?
jokes.setup: Everyone drinking from the new aluminum water bottles
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate recognition of the unpleasant metallic taste
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about aluminum bottle taste, with Michael's typical vivid description.

jokes.text: Take out your Suck It And you'll suck it, suck it Take out your Suck It and you'll suck it
jokes.setup: David and son performing jingle for their invention
jokes.punchline: Father and child singing repetitive song with unintentionally sexual lyrics
jokes.explanation: Culmination of the Suck It storyline - the innocent yet inappropriate jingle performance is memorable and cringey.
